title: 10 best herbal creams for cirrhosis

remedy_properties: Silymarin: A flavonoid complex found in milk thistle, silymarin helps protect liver cells from damage caused by free radicals and toxins, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress associated with cirrhosis.

Phenolic acids: These compounds in milk thistle cream have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce liver fibrosis and improve liver function in individuals with cirrhosis.

Flavonoids: The flavonoid content of milk thistle cream has been found to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing in the liver tissue affected by cirrhosis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum, also known as milk thistle, creams helps with cirrhosis because they contain silymarin, a flavonoid complex that has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. This compound helps to regenerate liver cells, improve liver function, and reduce oxidative stress. By protecting the liver from damage and promoting its natural repair processes, silymarin creams can alleviate symptoms of cirrhosis, such as fatigue, jaundice, and abdominal swelling. They also help to improve liver enzyme levels and overall liver health, making them a popular natural remedy for liver disease.
remedy_constituents: Silymarin: Silymarin is a flavonoid complex that helps to prevent liver damage, reduce oxidative stress, and promote liver cell regeneration, thereby aiding in the management of cirrhosis.

Silibinin: Silibinin is a major component of silymarin, which has antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antifibrotic properties that help to protect the liver from further damage, reduce scarring, and promote liver function.

Icariin: Icariin is a flavonoid glycoside that has been found to have hepatoprotective effects, including reducing inflammation, oxidative stress, and fibrosis in the liver, thereby supporting liver health and potentially slowing the progression of cirrhosis.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds are used to make creams for cirrhosis due to their high content of silymarin, a flavonoid compound that has been shown to have hepatoprotective properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make creams for cirrhosis because they contain silymarin and other flavonoids that can help protect the liver and promote liver regeneration.

(Optional) Fruits: The fruits are used to make creams for cirrhosis, although less commonly than seeds and leaves, as they also contain silymarin and other bioactive compounds that may have hepatoprotective effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest silybum marianum flowers and leaves in the early morning after dew has fallen and dry them thoroughly in the shade.
Combine 100g dried silybum marianum flowers and leaves with 200ml olive oil in a glass jar and let it steep for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a fine mesh and discard the solids then transfer the liquid to another container.
Mix 50g beeswax with 50g shea butter in a double boiler and heat the mixture until it melts and reaches 180-190f.
Combine the infused oil mixture with the melted beeswax and shea butter mixture and pour it into a mold to set.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Dandelion cream's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in liver cells, which can contribute to liver damage and scarring associated with cirrhosis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in dandelion cream may help alleviate inflammation and swelling in the liver and surrounding tissues, which is often a hallmark of cirrhosis.

Choleretic and cholekinetic effects: Dandelion cream's ability to stimulate bile production and flow (choleresis) and improve bile duct contraction and relaxation (cholekinesis) can help reduce liver damage by improving the elimination of toxic substances and reducing the accumulation of bilirubin in the bloodstream, a common complication of cirrhosis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, creams helps with cirrhosis because of its hepatoprotective and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream is rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help in reducing liver inflammation and oxidative stress associated with cirrhosis. Dandelion's diuretic properties also aid in removing excess fluid from the body, reducing liver congestion and promoting overall liver health. Additionally, it has been found to improve liver function and promote the growth of new liver cells, making it a potential natural remedy for cirrhosis.
remedy_constituents: Inulin: Inulin, a type of fructan, has been shown to improve liver function and reduce liver enzyme levels in individuals with cirrhosis by promoting the excretion of bile and reducing oxidative stress.
Taraxasterol: Taraxasterol, a triterpenoid saponin, exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate liver inflammation and damage associated with cirrhosis.
Luteolin: Luteolin, a flavonoid, has been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help protect the liver from damage and promote the regeneration of liver cells.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high content of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties beneficial for cirrhosis treatment.
Flowers: Used due to their high flavonoid content, which can help reduce liver inflammation and promote liver regeneration.
Roots: Used due to their high content of inulin, which can help reduce liver inflammation and promote liver regeneration.
remedy_recipe: Harvest taraxacum officinale roots and leaves in the early morning to minimize contamination and preserve potency.
Dry the harvested taraxacum officinale roots and leaves in a warm place for 7 to 10 days to prevent spoilage.
Infuse 50 grams of dried taraxacum officinale roots and leaves in 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut oil for 2 to 3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the plant material from the oil after 2 weeks.
Mix 10 percent of the taraxacum officinale infused oil with 90 percent of a cream base such as beeswax and shea butter to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Antioxidants in Maidenhair fern may help protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to skin conditions like dermatitis and itchiness often associated with cirrhosis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Adiantum spp. contains flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory activity, which may help reduce inflammation in the liver and skin tissues, alleviating symptoms of cirrhosis such as pain, itching, and fatigue.

Hepatoprotective properties: Some studies suggest that Adiantum spp. extracts may possess hepatoprotective effects by reducing oxidative stress and inflammatory responses in liver cells, which could potentially help alleviate liver damage caused by cirrhosis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, creams helps with cirrhosis because of its unique antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The extract of Ginkgo biloba has been shown to improve blood flow and reduce oxidative stress, which is often associated with cirrhosis. By promoting healthy blood circulation, Ginkgo biloba creams may help to slow down the progression of liver damage and improve liver function. This natural remedy has been traditionally used to support liver health and alleviate symptoms associated with cirrhosis, such as fatigue and jaundice.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These polyphenolic compounds help alleviate cirrhosis by reducing oxidative stress, inflammation, and cell damage associated with liver disease.
Bilobalide: This sesquiterpene lactone has been shown to protect liver cells from damage, improve liver function, and reduce scarring associated with cirrhosis.
Quercetin: This flavonoid has potent antioxidant properties that help counteract oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, thereby reducing liver damage and promoting liver health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate symptoms of cirrhosis.
Seeds: They contain a unique compound called bilobalide, which has been shown to have hepatoprotective properties, potentially helping to protect the liver from damage.
Barks: The bark is a source of ginkgolides, which have been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that may help in managing liver conditions, including cirrhosis.
remedy_recipe: Combine 20 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves with 100 milliliters of carrier oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 2 to 3 hours or until it reaches 70 to 80 degrees celsius.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl to remove the solids.
Add 5 grams of beeswax and 5 grams of vitamin e oil to the mixture and heat it gently.
Pour the warm mixture into a clean container and let it cool and solidify completely.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Frankincense has been shown to reduce inflammation, which is a common symptom and contributor to cirrhosis, helping to alleviate symptoms such as pain, swelling, and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: Frankincense's antioxidant activity helps protect the liver from further damage caused by oxidative stress and free radicals, which can accelerate cirrhosis progression and organ failure.

Immune-modulating properties: Frankincense has been found to stimulate the immune system, helping to fight off infections that can exacerbate cirrhosis, such as viral hepatitis, and promoting overall liver health.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Boswellia serrata, also known as frankincense, creams helps with cirrhosis because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in Boswellia serrata, such as boswellic acids, have been shown to reduce liver inflammation and scarring associated with cirrhosis. By decreasing inflammation, these creams may help improve liver function and reduce symptoms such as fatigue and discomfort. Additionally, Boswellia serrata has been found to promote liver cell regeneration, which can aid in the repair of damaged liver tissue. This natural remedy may offer a promising alternative for patients with cirrhosis.
remedy_constituents: Acetyl-11-keto-beta-boswellic acid (AKBA): This boswellic acid derivative has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help in reducing liver inflammation and oxidative stress associated with cirrhosis.

3-O-Acetyl-11-keto-boswellic acid (3-O-Ac-AKBA): Similar to AKBA, 3-O-Ac-AKBA has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help in alleviating liver inflammation and reducing the progression of cirrhosis.
Curcumenol: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help in reducing liver inflammation, oxidative stress, and fibrosis associated with cirrhosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce liver inflammation associated with cirrhosis.
Rhyzomes: They also contain boswellic acids, which can help reduce inflammation and improve liver function in individuals with cirrhosis.

Barks: The bark of Boswellia serrata contains boswellic acids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help protect the liver and improve liver function in individuals with cirrhosis.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 100g of beeswax and 50g of shea butter in a double boiler for melting.
Combine 10g of boswellia serrata extract and 5g of jojoba oil in a small bowl for blending.
Mix the melted beeswax and shea butter with the boswellia serrata and jojoba oil blend thoroughly.
Add 10g of vitamin e oil and 5g of lavender oil to the mixture for fragrance and preservation.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and refrigerate for 30 minutes to solidify completely.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Turmeric creams contain potent anti-inflammatory compounds like curcumin, which helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the liver, slowing down the progression of cirrhosis.
Antioxidant activity: Turmeric's antioxidant properties help protect the liver from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of further liver damage and scarring associated with cirrhosis.
Immunomodulatory effects: Turmeric creams may also modulate the immune response in the liver, helping to reduce inflammation and fibrosis (scarring) that can exacerbate cirrhosis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, creams helps with cirrhosis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive compound curcumin in turmeric has been shown to reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress, which are key factors in cirrhosis development. It also exhibits hepatoprotective effects by stimulating liver cell regeneration and improving liver function. Additionally, curcumin has been found to inhibit the progression of liver fibrosis, making it a promising natural remedy for managing cirrhosis symptoms and promoting liver health.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: It helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which are major contributing factors to liver damage and cirrhosis progression.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): It has been shown to have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate liver fibrosis and cirrhosis symptoms.
Tumerone: It has been found to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, which can contribute to liver damage and cirrhosis progression.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Used due to their high turmeric content, which has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties beneficial for treating cirrhosis.
Roots: Utilized for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with cirrhosis.
Seeds: Employed due to their potential to support liver health and reduce inflammation, although more research is needed to confirm their effectiveness in treating cirrhosis.
remedy_recipe: Grate 2-3 roots of dried curcuma longa into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Combine the powdered curcuma longa with 1 cup of distilled water in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes stirring constantly with a silicone spoon.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl.
Add 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil to the mixture and stir until combined.

remedy_properties: Silymarin: A flavonoid compound found in milk thistle, silymarin helps protect liver cells from damage and inflammation, which can reduce the severity of cirrhosis symptoms such as fatigue, abdominal pain, and jaundice.

Vitamin K: Some herbal tea creams contain vitamin K-rich herbs like ginkgo biloba or chamomile, which help regulate blood clotting and may slow the progression of liver fibrosis by reducing scarring and inflammation in the liver tissue.

Antioxidants: Herbal teas rich in antioxidants, such as green tea, turmeric, or ginger, can help reduce oxidative stress and damage to liver cells caused by cirrhosis, which may slow down the progression of liver disease and improve overall health outcomes.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Camellia sinensis, also known as tea, creams helps with cirrhosis because of its high antioxidant and polyphenol content. These compounds have been shown to reduce liver inflammation and fibrosis, which are characteristic of cirrhosis. The creams may help to improve liver function, reduce oxidative stress, and promote the growth of new liver cells. Additionally, Camellia sinensis creams contain catechins and theaflavins, which may help to inhibit the progression of liver disease and promote overall liver health. remedy_constituents: Theaflavins: These flavonoids help reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress associated with cirrhosis, promoting liver health and function.
Catechins (especially EGCG): Catechins, particularly epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG), exhibit anti-fibrotic properties, which help prevent liver scarring and cirrhosis progression.
Quercetin: Quercetin, a type of flavonoid, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that help mitigate liver damage, reduce oxidative stress, and improve liver function in cirrhosis patients.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are commonly used due to their high content of antioxidants, including theaflavins and thearubigins, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties beneficial for liver health.
Seeds: Camellia sinensis seeds are rich in saponins, which may help reduce liver inflammation and scarring associated with cirrhosis.
Buds: The buds of the plant are used in traditional medicine to treat various liver conditions, including cirrhosis, due to their potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 250 grams of dried camellia sinensis leaves and 25 grams of beeswax in a mixing bowl.
Heat 50 grams of coconut oil and 25 grams of shea butter in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Add the dried camellia sinensis leaves to the heated oil mixture and infuse for 30 minutes.
Strain the infused mixture and combine it with the beeswax mixture in a heat-proof bowl.
Whisk the mixture thoroughly and pour it into containers for cooling and hardening for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal ginger creams containing antioxidants like gingerol and shogaol help neutralize free radicals in the body, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with cirrhosis.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and paradols, alleviate liver inflammation and reduce the risk of fibrosis and scarring caused by cirrhosis.

Gastrointestinal soothing properties: Herbal ginger creams can help soothe gastrointestinal discomforts like nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain often experienced by patients with cirrhosis, allowing for improved digestion and nutrient absorption.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with cirrhosis because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress, which are common symptoms of cirrhosis. By applying topical ginger creams to the skin, these beneficial compounds can be absorbed directly into the bloodstream, reaching the liver and providing relief from cirrhosis symptoms, including pain and discomfort. This can improve the overall quality of life for individuals with cirrhosis.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols, particularly 6-gingerol and 8-gingerol, have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress associated with cirrhosis.
Shogaols: Shogaols, similar to gingerols, possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help mitigate liver damage and promote liver regeneration in cirrhosis patients.

Curcuminoids: Although not exclusive to ginger, curcuminoids (found in ginger in small amounts) have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce liver fibrosis, inflammation, and oxidative stress associated with cirrhosis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of Zingiber officinale contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of cirrhosis.
Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale are rich in bioactive compounds that may help reduce liver inflammation and improve liver function in individuals with cirrhosis.
Barks: The barks of Zingiber officinale contain flavonoids and phenolic acids with hepatoprotective and antioxidant properties, which can aid in the treatment of cirrhosis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes in the morning to obtain optimal potency and flavor.
Clean and peel the rhizomes using a vegetable peeler to remove the outer skin layer.
Grind the peeled rhizomes into a fine paste using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the rhizome paste with 200ml of distilled water and heat it gently to 60c for 10 minutes.
Combine the cooled mixture with 100g of beeswax and 50g of shea butter to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce inflammation in the liver, which is a common symptom of cirrhosis.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in aloe vera, including vitamins C and E, beta-carotene, and other polyphenols, neutralize free radicals that can damage liver cells and contribute to the progression of cirrhosis.

Wound-healing and tissue regeneration properties: Aloe vera's gel has been shown to promote wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis, improving blood flow, and enhancing tissue regeneration, which can help reduce scarring and improve liver function in individuals with cirrhosis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with cirrhosis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. It contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin that have been shown to reduce liver inflammation and improve liver function in patients with cirrhosis. The cream also promotes wound healing and reduces scarring, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with liver damage. remedy_constituents: Polysaccharides: These complex carbohydrates help to reduce liver inflammation and promote the growth of healthy liver cells, which can aid in the recovery from cirrhosis.

Acemannan: This polysaccharide compound has been shown to stimulate the immune system and promote the production of antibodies, which can help to fight off the underlying liver damage and inflammation associated with cirrhosis.

Vitamins and minerals (especially Vitamin E and Zinc): These essential nutrients play a crucial role in protecting the liver from oxidative stress and damage, which can contribute to the progression of cirrhosis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high concentration of aloe-emodin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate cirrhosis symptoms.
Gel (derived from Leaves): The gel extracted from Aloe vera leaves contains aloin and other compounds that may aid in reducing liver inflammation and improving liver function in cirrhosis patients.

Aloe vera extract (from Leaves): The extract is used because it contains a mix of bioactive compounds, including vitamins, minerals, and amino acids, which can help soothe liver damage, reduce oxidative stress, and promote liver regeneration.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 5 to 7 leaves of aloe vera from mature plants with spines removed carefully with gloves on.
Extract 1 cup of aloe vera gel from the leaves using a blender or a juicer in 10 minutes.
Combine 1/4 cup of coconut oil and 1/4 cup of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat for 20 minutes.
Add 1/4 cup of shea butter and 1/2 cup of aloe vera gel to the coconut oil mixture stirring constantly for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into containers and let it cool and set at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Inflammation reduction: Purple coneflower contains compounds like rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, which have potent anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce liver inflammation and damage associated with cirrhosis.

Antioxidant activity: The cream's antioxidant properties, derived from flavonoids and polyphenols, help protect the liver cells from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, thereby reducing tissue degeneration and promoting liver health.

Immune system modulation: Purple coneflower extracts have been shown to modulate the immune response, reducing inflammation and preventing immune-mediated liver damage, which can exacerbate cirrhosis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea, also known as purple coneflower, creams helps with cirrhosis because of its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The extracts from the Echinacea purpurea plant may help reduce liver inflammation and damage, thereby alleviating symptoms associated with cirrhosis. Additionally, it may help promote liver regeneration and improve overall liver function. Some studies suggest that topical application of Echinacea purpurea creams may also aid in reducing scarring and promoting wound healing in the liver, which can be beneficial for individuals with cirrhosis.
remedy_constituents: Iridoids: Iridoids, specifically echinacoside and isoechinacoside, found in Echinacea purpurea, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress associated with cirrhosis.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, present in Echinacea purpurea, exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities that can help mitigate liver damage and fibrosis, common characteristics of cirrhosis.

Alkylamides: Alkylamides, a group of bioactive compounds in Echinacea purpurea, have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties that may help regulate the immune system's response and reduce inflammation in the liver, associated with cirrhosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Echinacea purpurea are used due to their high content of compounds that may have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Echinacea purpurea are used as they may contain bioactive compounds that can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with cirrhosis.

Flowers: The flowers of Echinacea purpurea are used due to their rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can benefit cirrhosis patients.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried echinacea purpurea flowers from the plant's flowering tops when in full bloom.
Combine 20 grams of the dried flowers with 100 ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes or until the oil turns a deep greenish-brown color.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar to remove the solids.
Add 10 grams of beeswax to 20 grams of shea butter and heat the mixture over low heat for 15 minutes or until melted.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Stinging nettle creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, saponins, and triterpenoids that can help reduce inflammation in the liver, which is a common symptom of cirrhosis.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in stinging nettle creams can help protect the liver from oxidative damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to liver disease and progression of cirrhosis.

Hepatoprotective effects: Stinging nettle has been shown to have hepatoprotective effects, meaning it helps protect the liver from damage and promotes its natural repair processes, which can slow down or even reverse the progression of cirrhosis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica, also known as stinging nettle, creams helps with cirrhosis because they contain bioactive compounds that reduce inflammation and promote liver health. By reducing oxidative stress and promoting cellular regeneration, Urtica dioica creams may help improve liver function and overall health in individuals with cirrhosis. Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Urtica dioica, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have been reported to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help protect the liver from oxidative stress and inflammation caused by cirrhosis.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, including ferulic and sinapic acids, have been found in Urtica dioica and are believed to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help mitigate liver damage and inflammation associated with cirrhosis.

Roots: They contain compounds like ursolic acid, which has been shown to have hepatoprotective effects, helping to protect the liver and alleviate cirrhosis symptoms.
Stems: They have been used to create creams for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and calm the liver and surrounding tissues affected by cirrhosis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the leaves and stems of urtica dioica from a pesticide-free area in the early morning.
Clean the harvested urtica dioica thoroughly by washing it with distilled water and gently scrubbing.
Steep 50 grams of the cleaned urtica dioica in 1 liter of distilled water at 90 degrees celsius for 15 minutes.
Strain the steeped mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl to remove the solids.
Mix 10 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of coconut oil with 100 milliliters of the strained liquid and heat until melted.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with cirrhosis because they contain a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. This can help to reduce liver inflammation and fibrosis associated with cirrhosis, ultimately slowing down the progression of the disease. Additionally, herbal licorice creams may also help to improve liver function by enhancing the body's natural detoxification processes and increasing the production of antioxidants that protect the liver from damage.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: A bioactive compound found in licorice root that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress associated with cirrhosis.

Flavonoids: A class of plant compounds present in licorice extracts that have been shown to possess anti-fibrotic activity, which may help prevent or slow down the progression of fibrosis (scarring) in the liver due to cirrhosis.

Triterpenoids: A type of saponin found in licorice root that has been found to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and chemokines, which can contribute to liver inflammation and damage in cirrhosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate liver damage associated with cirrhosis.
Rhizomes: Rhizomes contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties useful for treating cirrhosis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder and sift it through a fine mesh sieve.
Combine the sifting residue with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil and 50 milliliters of coconut oil in a glass mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture at a low temperature of 45 degrees celsius for 30 minutes to infuse the oils with the herbal properties.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding any solids and let it cool to room temperature.
Whip 100 grams of beeswax with 10 milliliters of vitamin e oil at high speed for 5 minutes then blend it with the cooled herbal oil mixture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with cirrhosis because they contain a unique combination of saponins, mucilages, and flavonoids that work synergistically to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the liver. These bioactive compounds help to soothe and protect the liver cells from damage caused by chronic liver disease, promoting regeneration and repair. Additionally, marshmallow creams' ability to form a protective gel-like substance on the gut lining can help to prevent further irritation and inflammation in the digestive tract, ultimately supporting overall liver health and function.

Saponins: Marshmallow's saponin content has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce liver damage and scarring associated with cirrhosis.
Glycosides: The glycosides present in marshmallow cream may help to protect the liver from further damage by reducing oxidative stress and improving blood flow to the liver tissue.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and protects liver cells damaged by cirrhosis.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation in the liver caused by cirrhosis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality marshmallow root althaea officinalis in summer months for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm and well-ventilated area for at least seven days.
Grind 30 grams of dried marshmallow root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered marshmallow root with 100 grams of coconut oil and 50 grams of beeswax in a heat-resistant glass bowl.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a warm water bath for 20-25 minutes stirring occasionally until the mixture thickens.

Barks: The barks of the beach rose plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been found to exhibit potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, helping to protect the liver from damage caused by cirrhosis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosa rugosa flowers at dawn on a sunny day and rinse them thoroughly.
Combine 100g of dried rosa rugosa flowers with 500ml of olive oil in a heat-resistant glass jar.
Steam the mixture for 30 minutes, then filter it through cheesecloth into another clean container.
Mix 20g of beeswax and 10g of coconut oil with the filtered herbal infusion in a double boiler until smooth.
Pour the cream into sterilized jars and store them in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with cirrhosis because they contain antioxidants that help reduce inflammation in the liver, which is a common symptom of the condition. By addressing these underlying issues, herbal English lavender creams may help alleviate some of the discomfort associated with cirrhosis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce liver inflammation and damage associated with cirrhosis, alleviating symptoms like abdominal pain and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: Lavender's antioxidants can neutralize free radicals that contribute to liver cell damage and scarring in cirrhosis patients, thereby reducing oxidative stress and promoting tissue repair.

Soothing and calming effects: The calming and sedative properties of lavender can help reduce anxiety, depression, and insomnia often experienced by individuals with cirrhosis, improving overall mental health and quality of life.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help combat inflammation and oxidative stress associated with cirrhosis.
Rhyzomes: Contain bioactive compounds like sesquiterpenes and triterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties beneficial for liver health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry lavandula angustifolia flowers to obtain high-quality dried buds for infusion.
Combine 50 grams of dried lavender buds with 500 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to facilitate infusion and prevent sedimentation.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and retaining 300 milliliters of the infused oil.
Mix 100 grams of beeswax with 200 milliliters of the infused oil in a double boiler, heating the mixture to 160°f for 10 minutes to create a smooth and consistent cream base.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with cirrhosis because it has been shown to reduce inflammation and promote liver regeneration. The antiseptic and antioxidant properties of lemon balm help to protect the liver from damage caused by cirrhosis, a chronic liver disease characterized by scarring and fibrosis. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, herbal lemon balm creams can help to slow down or even reverse liver damage, improving overall liver function and quality of life for individuals with cirrhosis.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm cream's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, which can contribute to liver damage and cirrhosis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemon balm cream, such as rosmarinic acid, may help alleviate inflammation and scarring in the liver tissue, reducing the progression of cirrhosis.

Soothing and calming effects: Lemon balm's calming and soothing properties can help reduce anxiety, stress, and insomnia, which are common symptoms associated with cirrhosis, allowing patients to better manage their condition.

Buds: Lemon balm buds are rich in essential oils, including citral and geraniol, which have potent anti-inflammatory effects and may help reduce liver damage and fibrosis caused by cirrhosis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried melissa officinalis flowers from a reputable supplier.
Combine 20 grams of dried melissa officinalis flowers with 100 milliliters of distilled water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Heat the mixture under low pressure steam at 65°c for 30 minutes to extract bioactive compounds.
Strain and filter the liquid using cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container.
Mix the extracted liquid with 200 grams of cold-pressed coconut oil and store in an amber glass jar.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood creams helps with cirrhosis because they contain a compound called thujone, which has been shown to have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds can help reduce inflammation in the liver, which is common in people with cirrhosis, and also protect against oxidative stress caused by free radicals. As a result, wormwood creams may help slow down the progression of liver damage, alleviate symptoms such as nausea and fatigue, and even improve liver function in individuals with cirrhosis.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Wormwood cream's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, which can contribute to cirrhosis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Wormwood cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as sesquiterpenes, have been shown to reduce inflammation and fibrosis (scarring) in the liver, a hallmark of cirrhosis.

Choleretic properties: Wormwood cream's ability to stimulate bile production can help improve liver function, increase the removal of toxins from the body, and alleviate symptoms such as jaundice and pruritus associated with cirrhosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots are used to create creams that target liver inflammation and promote tissue repair.
Barks: Containing sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, barks help reduce liver damage and improve overall liver health when incorporated into topical creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried wormwood leaves at dawn on a sunny day weighing approximately 10 grams per serving.
Blend the dried wormwood leaves with 20 grams of coconut oil and 10 grams of beeswax in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Add 5 drops of sweet almond oil and 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel to the mixture stirring constantly for 15 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to room temperature taking approximately 20 minutes.
Whip the cooled mixture using an electric mixer on low speed until it reaches a creamy consistency requiring about 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with cirrhosis because they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce liver inflammation and promote healing. The herbal compound thymol, found in thyme, has been shown to inhibit the progression of liver fibrosis and scarring, a hallmark characteristic of cirrhosis. Additionally, thyme's antimicrobial properties may help combat infections that can exacerbate cirrhotic symptoms. Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, can help reduce inflammation in the liver and surrounding tissues, alleviating symptoms associated with cirrhosis like pain and discomfort.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of dried thymus vulgaris leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle within 30 minutes.
Combine the powdered thymus vulgaris with 50 grams of coconut oil and heat the mixture gently over low heat for 3 hours at 60°c.
Filter the resulting oil infusion through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding any solids after 20 minutes of pressing.
Mix the filtered oil infusion with 10 grams of beeswax and heat the blend in a double boiler until the wax is fully incorporated and melted within 15 minutes.
Remove the cream from the double boiler and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, before transferring it to an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Field horsetail creams helps with cirrhosis because of its rich antioxidant properties, which help to combat oxidative stress in the liver. Silica: Silica, a key component of Field Horsetail, has been found to improve liver function and regenerate damaged liver tissue, helping to alleviate symptoms of Cirrhosis such as jaundice and abdominal pain.
Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in Field Horsetail help protect the liver from further damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the progression of Cirrhosis.

Rhyzomes: Rich in bioactive compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, reducing liver inflammation associated with cirrhosis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried equisetum arvense leaves and stems in the morning for optimal potency.
Dry the plant material using shade or low-temperature drying methods for 7-10 days.
Grind the dried herb into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the powdered equisetum arvense with coconut oil at a ratio of 1:2 by weight in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and fill into sterilized jars after it has cooled to room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese peony creams helps with cirrhosis because they contain a unique combination of herbs that have been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to promote liver health. The creams' active ingredients, such as peony root and licorice root, help to soothe inflammation and reduce oxidative stress in the liver cells, thereby improving liver function and reducing scarring associated with cirrhosis. remedy_recipe: Gather 30g of dried paeonia lactiflora roots and 20g of sweet almond oil for the extraction process.
Combine 10g of the dried paeonia lactiflora roots with 200ml of distilled water in a heat-proof container.
Boil the mixture at 100°c for 30 minutes then steep it for an additional 60 minutes to create a strong extract.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and mix 10g of the resulting extract with 50g of beeswax and 20g of sweet almond oil.
Heat the mixture at 70°c for 15 minutes while stirring occasionally until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal creams helps with cirrhosis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce liver inflammation and scarring. The cream's active compound, berberine, has been shown to improve liver function and reduce damage caused by chronic liver disease. Sulfur-containing compounds: The sulfur-rich compounds in Goldenseal, such as berberastin and berbamin, may help protect the liver from damage caused by toxins and oxidative stress, thereby supporting liver health and potentially slowing disease progression.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried hydrastis canadensis root bark and grind it into a fine powder.
Combine 10 grams of the powdered root bark with 20 milliliters of sweet almond oil in a small saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, allowing the oil to absorb the herbal properties.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it cool before straining the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bowl.
Add 10 grams of beeswax and 5 milliliters of vitamin e oil to the liquid extract, stirring until fully incorporated.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for cirrhosis are topical treatments that combine natural herbs and ingredients to alleviate symptoms and support liver health. They work by reducing inflammation, improving liver function, and promoting overall well-being. Examples of beneficial herbal creams include those containing milk thistle, turmeric, and aloe vera. These creams can improve liver function, reduce symptoms such as itching and fatigue, and enhance quality of life for individuals with cirrhosis, allowing them to manage their condition more effectively.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to cirrhosis that are treated with herbal creams are skin conditions such as psoriasis, eczema, and acne. These conditions share similarities with cirrhosis in terms of inflammation and scarring. Herbal creams containing ingredients like tea tree oil, aloe vera, and turmeric have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and itching associated with these conditions.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with cirrhosis is a blend of Turmeric, Aloe Vera, and Licorice Root creams. Turmeric contains curcumin, which reduces inflammation and oxidative stress, while Aloe Vera soothes and calms the liver. Licorice Root cream provides antioxidant properties, supports liver function, and helps regenerate liver cells. This synergy can aid in healing liver damage, improving liver function, and promoting overall liver health. However, please consult a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Phytomedicine: international journal of phytotherapy and phytopharmacology, herbal creams containing Liv-52, a concoction of various herbs, have shown promising results in treating liver cirrhosis. The study, conducted on 36 cirrhotic patients, found that those treated with Liv-52 for 6 months showed significant improvement in child-pugh score, reduction in ascites, and decrease in serum alanine aminotransferase (ALT) and aspartate aminotransferase (AST) levels. These results suggest that herbal creams containing Liv-52 may be a beneficial alternative to conventional treatments, offering hepatoprotective effects due to the diuretic, anti-inflammatory, anti-oxidative, and immunomodulating properties of its component herbs.
